puzzle0.title: POD BEACON ALIGNMENT
puzzle0.description: Match each rescue system with the correct stabilization target.

puzzle1.title: POD ACCESS CORRELATION
puzzle1.description: Match each pod access record with the correct conclusion.

event2.text: Beacon lock unstable. Pod trajectory is drifting.

event7.text: Or I heard something wearing his voice.

event9.text: Don't tell me it wasn't him yet.

event11.text: I know that is irrational. I am choosing to be aware of it and still furious.

event18.text: Nobody touches that hatch until we know what is moving inside it.

event6.text: That does not make the thing attached to it safer.

event10.text: But it means we can hear it better.

event13.text: That sounded less comforting out loud.

event1.text: We do not have much time. And before anyone asks, no, I do not have a calm version of that sentence.

event8.text: Said they were just clocks with an ego.

event10.text: He would absolutely hate this one.

event0.title: RESCUE ROUTE MAP
event0.content:
ROUTE A
Short Route
High Radiation
Estimated Arrival: Fast
Crew Risk: Severe
Pod Life Support Risk: Moderate

ROUTE B
Long Route
Low Radiation
Estimated Arrival: Delayed
Crew Risk: Moderate
Pod Life Support Risk: Severe
event0.caption: Recovered rescue route analysis. Both routes carry significant trade-offs.
event0.message: [RESCUE ROUTE MAP ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event3.text: Route A is fast and radioactive.

event7.text: Exactly how I wanted to die today.

event9.text: Route B is slower but less likely to cook you.

event11.text: It is deeply annoying that your argument is good.

event13.text: But if that life support drops much further, slow becomes a decision too.

backgroundEvent0.text: Fast route. Terrible route. Very us.

backgroundEvent1.text: Keep moving. Do not stop in the exposed corridor.

backgroundEvent5.text: You know, Ethan, your concern sounds almost human when it isn't buried under orders.

backgroundEvent1.text: Not for whoever is in that pod.

backgroundEvent5.text: Alive people are allowed to keep themselves alive.

backgroundEvent6.text: That sounds like something you say after leaving someone behind.

backgroundEvent2.text: Do not let Hale open it first.

event4.text: Relay to pod. Guidance to trajectory. Life support to biometrics.

event6.text: Try again before the pod decides to navigate by spite.

event1.title: POD INTERNAL SCAN REPORT
event1.content:
Visual Feed: unavailable
Interior Motion: intermittent
Thermal Bloom: one occupant-sized mass
Suit Seal Trace: present
Occupant Identity: unresolved
Scan Distortion: recursive signal folding detected
event1.caption: Interior scan reconstructed without image data. Occupant cannot be visually identified.
event1.message: [POD INTERNAL SCAN REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event9.text: The scan is trying not to show us a person.

event11.text: Or trying very hard to invent one.

event1.text: The scan keeps folding over itself, like the system is trying not to show us a person.

event6.text: Noah used to say cameras lie less than people.

event10.text: Cameras lie all the time. They just do it with confidence.

event1.text: Which is probably a bad sign about my decision-making, but a good sign for whoever is inside.

event6.text: If this is a trap, I am going to be very annoyed at how emotionally effective it is.
Maya, if you open that pod, you may not be rescuing anyone.

event8.text: You may be letting something out.

event14.text: The part where you finally say what you really think.

event1.text: For the record, I hate that this is the smart thing to do.

event8.text: He said waiting was just fear pretending to be organized.

event6.text: Don't ask me how I forgot saying that until now.

event1.text: Before Alpha. The pod was empty.

event2.text: No body. No blood. No suit. Just frost on the inside of the glass.

event19.text: That is such an Ethan answer.

backgroundEvent23.text: Then why didn't you tell us?

backgroundEvent24.text: Because I thought empty meant safe.

event0.title: OLD POD ACCESS LOG
event0.content:
Access Event: Emergency Pod Manual Inspection
Timestamp: Before Incident Alpha
Operator: Ethan Hale
Pod State: empty
Interior Residue: frost / no biological matter detected
Seal Reclosure: completed manually
Conflict: current pod telemetry reports active biometric motion
event0.caption: Recovered old pod access record. Hale previously opened the same pod before Alpha.
event0.message: [OLD POD ACCESS LOG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event11.text: Or something was always there and the system learned not to see it.

event1.text: Rescue team has reached emergency pod exterior.

event3.title: POD ACCESS RISK REPORT
event3.content:
Internal Motion: detected
Seal Pressure: unstable
Manual Override: required
Audio Channel Contamination: rising
External Voice Traffic: unauthorized
Recommendation: correlate access records before opening
event3.caption: Recovered pod access risk report.
event3.message: [POD ACCESS RISK REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event11.text: Everything terrible on this station eventually becomes a handle someone has to pull.

event4.text: Motion means something is inside now.

event6.text: The old log means I opened it before Alpha.

event8.text: Seal pressure means manual override. Separate the facts before you open the hatch.

event4.text: The hatch accepts the override path.

event6.text: The pod does not open yet.

backgroundEvent2.text: Maya, step away from it. Now.

backgroundEvent5.text: Operator, I can't walk away from that.

event3.text: Relay array stabilizes the pod beacon.

event7.text: Life support trace follows occupant biometrics.

event10.text: Do not mix them just because everything is screaming at once.

event3.text: Old pod access log: I opened it before Alpha.

event5.text: Seal pressure report: manual override required.

event8.text: Do not make this simpler than it is.

event10.text: Simple is how traps get polite.
